description.value:
Category: Taijutsu, Rare

Prerequisite: Taijutsu Ability Score of 16+

You have begun to master the use of the Eight Inner Gates.

A legendary technique designed to release the limits on a user's chakra pathways, thus increasing their physical strength and speed.

You learn to unlock 3 of such Inner gates; Kaimon, Kyumon and Seimon.

Unlocking each gate grants additional benefits in addition to the benefits granted by the previous gate activated.

Gates cannot be unlocked out of order.

Activating each Inner Gate costs an Action, Bonus Action or reaction on your turn.

While you are gaining the benefit of any Gate you cannot cast or maintain the concentration on any Ninjutsu or Genjutsu.

Any Gate you activate can only remain active for 1 minute or until you deactivate it.

You must complete a rest to gain the benefit of any Gate granted by this feat.

If you would attempt to gain the benefit of a Gate granted by this feat a second time without taking a rest, you instead must spend 2 hit die to do so, which are not recovered until you complete a full-rest.

When you would end the activation of or be forced to end any 8-Inner gate, you gain all Ranks of any Fatigue.

You lose one ranks of Fatigue every 1 hour;

• Kaimon.

- Str: +2 o Speed: +15 o +1 Rank of Fatigue

• Kyumon.

- Str: +2 o Speed: +15 o Spend 2 Hit Die, regaining the result. This can only be used once per short rest.

- +2 Rank of Fatigue

• Seimon.

- Str: +4 o Speed: +10 o AC: +1 o +2 Rank(s) of Fatigue.
